caption:
6 edge runtime patterns every Next.js dev should know.

Vercel Edge, Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy — your API runs in 300+ locations worldwide. 0ms cold start. But the rules are different.

📌 SAVE this edge runtime reference.

Are you deploying to the edge yet? 👇

#nextjs #vercel #cloudflare #edge #webdev #coding #programming #developer #serverless #codingtips

slides.title: Edge runtime patterns every Next.js dev should know
slides.subtitle: 0ms cold start. 300+ locations. Different rules.

slides.title: Edge Config — instant global config
slides.desc: Read configuration in <1ms from the edge. No database round trip.
slides.punchline: Feature flags at the speed of light.

slides.title: Middleware — intercept every request
slides.desc: Runs before your page loads. Auth, redirects, A/B tests — globally.

slides.title: Streaming — send HTML as it's ready
slides.desc: Don't wait for the whole page. Stream components as they resolve.

slides.title: KV Store — key-value at the edge
slides.desc: Cloudflare KV or Vercel KV. Read globally in <10ms.
slides.punchline: Global cache. No Redis cluster to manage.

slides.title: Geo routing — personalize by location
slides.desc: req.geo gives you country, city, latitude, longitude. Free.

slides.title: Limits — what you can't do
slides.desc: Edge ≠ Node.js. Know the constraints before you deploy.

slides.title: Edge Runtime Cheat Sheet

items.label: Limits
items.desc: No fs, no native Node APIs

slides.question: Are you deploying to the edge? 👇
